summary: Backend + Frontend E2E testing. 17/21 pytest cases pass. Public + auth + thread CRUD + auth gating + stats all working correctly. CRITICAL BLOCKER: /api/ask is failing with 500 (returned to client as 502 by ingress) because the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY's per-key budget cap ($0.001) has been exhausted. LiteLLM raises BudgetExceededError -> emergentintegrations raises ChatError -> the unhandled exception propagates through asyncio.gather() in run_agents and crashes the LangGraph pipeline. This breaks the entire ask flow, semantic cache, thread persistence-after-ask, agent traces, and dashboard metric growth. Frontend UI renders correctly across landing/login/register/chat/dashboard and all data-testids exist. Invalid login error is shown correctly. The first manually-verified /api/ask succeeded earlier (admin's run logged scores=[10,10,10,2]); the budget got exhausted shortly after, so subsequent ask calls now uniformly fail.

critical.issue: Fails with 500 (ingress -> 502) when LLM budget is exceeded. emergentintegrations raises ChatError('Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0014256, Max budget: 0.001') and the exception propagates out of asyncio.gather(*tasks) in agents/graph.py:run_agents -> entire pipeline aborts, no answer persisted. No per-agent error trapping.

asyncio.gather() called WITHOUT return_exceptions=True; a single failing agent (rate limit, arxiv timeout, tavily error, etc.) kills all 4 agents and the user gets a 500 with no graceful fallback. Spec explicitly says we should still produce a graceful fallback when tavily is disabled or arxiv is slow.

minor.issue: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/ live INSIDE the uvicorn watch path, so editing them triggers server reload mid-test. Recommend keeping tests under /app/tests or excluding /app/backend/tests from --reload-dir. (Tests still ran; this just adds flakiness.)

EmailStr normalises case (email is lowercased server-side via .lower().strip()) which is correct, but the response/me also returns lowercase regardless of the casing the user typed. Acceptable; just noting for clarity.

integration_issues.issue: Because /api/ask returns 500/502, the UI will surface a network error. Could not validate cache-hit-badge, agent-trace-panel, message-assistant-N, toggle-trace-N end-to-end because no new assistant message can be created until LLM budget is fixed.

action_items: URGENT: Rotate/refresh EMERGENT_LLM_KEY or raise its budget cap. Current key ($0.001 cap) is exhausted so the entire 4-agent pipeline returns 500/502.

Make run_agents() resilient: use asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True) and replace exceptions with a graceful AgentTrace{answer='<agent_name> error: ...', score=0, elapsed_ms=elapsed}. The evaluator/refiner should then continue with the surviving agents.
Add a try/except around call_llm in _run_agent so a single LLM failure becomes a low-score trace instead of a pipeline crash.
Wrap evaluate() and refine() with the same defensive pattern so a judge/refiner LLM failure doesn't 500 the whole request - fall back to picking the highest-scored (or longest) candidate.
Move backend test file out of the uvicorn watch path (e.g. keep it at /app/tests/backend_test.py) so editing it doesn't restart the backend.
Consider returning a friendly 503 with detail='LLM provider rate-limited, retry shortly' instead of letting ChatError bubble to a 500.
critical_code_review_comments: agents/graph.py:137 - asyncio.gather(*tasks) lacks return_exceptions=True; this is the single biggest robustness bug.
agents/graph.py - No try/except in _run_agent, evaluate, refine. Any LLM error 500s the API.
chat/routes.py:18 - module-level _user_caches dict will grow unbounded across users; fine for demo but flag for production (LRU cap).
agents/cache.py - per-user TfidfVectorizer is hydrated on first request; first ask after restart incurs a hydrate cost. OK but document it.
auth/routes.py - seed_admin updates the admin password only if it differs; good. forgot-password prints reset link to logs - acceptable for demo, mark as MOCKED for prod.
server.py:62 - relies on os.environ['DB_NAME'] (KeyError if missing); since .env supplies it, fine, but worth a clearer error message.
stats/routes.py - aggregations are fine but no cap on agent_runs scan size (could be slow if many docs).

success_rate.backend: 81% (17/21 pytest, with 4 failures all attributable to one root cause: LLM budget exhaustion)
success_rate.frontend: 95% (landing/login/register/chat shell/dashboard render correctly; ask flow blocked by backend)

test_credentials: admin@decision-engine.dev / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md - works). New users register fine via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Tests created throwaway users with prefix test_user_<uuid>@example.com. No cleanup performed (acceptable, low volume).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All auth, thread, stats endpoints verified working. Re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py after main agent (a) refreshes/raises EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget AND (b) hardens run_agents/evaluate/refine with return_exceptions=True + per-agent try/except. After fix, focus on the ask -> assistant message -> cache hit on repeat -> persistence after restart -> agent trace panel UI flows (data-testids: message-assistant-0, toggle-trace-0, cache-hit-badge-0, agent-trace-panel). Browser smoke for landing/login/dashboard already proven; no need to repeat unless code changed.
rca of the issue: Root cause: backend logs show `emergentintegrations.llm.chat.ChatError: Failed to generate chat completion: litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0014256, Max budget: 0.001`. The EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env has a $0.001 budget cap that was consumed by manual verification + the first integration test. Once exceeded, every gpt-4.1-mini call inside _run_agent raises ChatError. Because run_agents() uses `await asyncio.gather(*tasks)` without `return_exceptions=True`, the first ChatError aborts the whole pipeline and FastAPI returns 500 (Kubernetes ingress wraps it as 502). Reproduction: POST /api/ask with any question while the key is over budget => 500. Mitigation: (1) rotate EMERGENT_LLM_KEY or increase budget; (2) make agent fan-out resilient by capturing per-task exceptions and treating them as low-score (0) traces, so evaluator/refiner can still produce an answer from any surviving agent.
